subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Join the Theater Readers as they prepare a clear, lively read-aloud for the school broadcast.
lesson.objective: I can read grade-level text accurately, smoothly, and with expression.
lesson.concept_explanation: Good readers read grade-level text accurately, smoothly, and with expression. They slow down, notice clues in the words or text, and explain how the clue supports the answer.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers sometimes skip small words and change the meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: Fluency helps when you read directions, perform poems, share reports, and understand chapter books.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read every word accurately, group words into phrases, pause at punctuation, and reread to improve smoothness and expression.
lesson.mini_lesson: First read for accuracy. Next group words that belong together. Then let commas, periods, question marks, and exclamation marks guide your voice. Reread to make it smoother.

audio.text: accuracy, phrasing, expression, punctuation, repeated reading
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: The reader says every word in “The clever rabbit slipped under the garden gate” and does not skip clever or under.
worked_examples.correct_answer: The clever rabbit slipped under the garden gate.
worked_examples.sentence: The clever rabbit slipped under the garden gate.

audio.text: The clever rabbit slipped under the garden gate.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: “After lunch / the students / returned to the classroom” sounds smooth because the pauses keep word groups together.
worked_examples.correct_answer: After lunch / the students / returned to the classroom.
worked_examples.sentence: After lunch the students returned to the classroom.

audio.text: After lunch, the students returned to the classroom.
audio.label: Listen

hint_ladder: Track every word with your eyes.
Look for phrase groups and punctuation.
Reread the sentence so it sounds like talking.

misconception_bank.skips_words: Readers sometimes skip small words and change the meaning.
misconception_bank.punctuation_ignored: Readers may ignore punctuation and miss how the sentence should sound.
misconception_bank.phrase_chunking_error: Readers may pause in the middle of word groups that belong together.
misconception_bank.expression_flat_reading: Readers may read every sentence in the same flat voice.

story.text: Welcome to Reading Fluency and Expression. In this mission, you will read grade-level text accurately, smoothly, and with expression while helping a reader solve a real classroom task. Listen to the story goal, look for the reading strategy, and Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches you how to read every word, group phrases smoothly, and let punctuation guide expression. Read the objective, look at the visual model, and think about the watch-out mistake before answering. Press Next to continue after you can say the strategy in your own words.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example carefully because it shows the whole thinking path. Step one, read the prompt or passage. Step two, use the model to find the clue. Step three, check that the final answer matches the text before you Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo lets you try the strategy with support. Listen to the example, look at the model, and think about why the answer fits. Press Next to continue when you are ready for independent practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is your Practice zone for Reading Fluency and Expression. Use Read Question to hear the prompt, then reread the sentence or passage yourself. Choose your answer when you are ready, and Use the hint if you need help.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This Challenge zone asks you to apply the same strategy with a little more independence. Use Read Question first, listen for any helpful audio, and check the model before answering. Answer carefully, then continue once your choice matches the text.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This Checkpoint shows what you can do after the lesson and challenge. Use Read Question to hear the prompt, then prove your answer with the reading strategy. Answer carefully, then continue to see how your work earns growth credit.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You reached the badge screen for Reading Fluency and Expression. Think about one strategy you used well, such as rereading, checking a clue, or proving an answer. Press Next to see your profile and keep building your Grade 3 English skills.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page summarizes your growth for Reading Fluency and Expression. Read your accuracy, check any misconception notes, and notice the recommended next step. Continue to Skill Practice or go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
